title: linum alpinum subsp. alpinum
intro: Linum alpinum subsp. alpinum, also known as Alpine flax, is a plant species in the family Linaceae that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Alpine flax is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
